title: BME280 Environmental Sensor
desc: Precision I2C environmental sensor that measures temperature, humidity, and barometric pressure for ESP32 weather, altitude, and climate projects.

title: DHT22 Temperature & Humidity Sensor
desc: Digital temperature and humidity sensor for ESP32 weather stations, room monitors, greenhouse controllers, and climate automation projects.

title: ESP32 DevKit Board
desc: Beginner-friendly ESP32 development board with USB programming, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, 3.3 V GPIO, and enough pins for real hardware projects.

title: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
desc: Low-cost ultrasonic distance sensor that measures nearby objects with sound pulses for ESP32 robots, parking aids, and level sensing.

title: HC-SR501 PIR Motion Sensor
desc: Passive infrared motion sensor module that gives an ESP32 a digital HIGH signal when warm objects move through its field of view.

title: 1-Channel Relay Module
desc: Electrically isolated switch module that lets an ESP32 control higher-power DC loads and carefully supervised AC loads from a low-current GPIO signal.

title: SSD1306 OLED Display
desc: Small monochrome I2C OLED display for showing ESP32 sensor readings, menus, status messages, icons, and debugging information.

title: Analog Inputs
desc: Buttons teach the ESP32 yes or no. Analog inputs teach it how much: how bright, how loud, how wet, how far, or where a dial is turned.

title: Analog Inputs
desc: Buttons teach the ESP32 yes or no. Analog inputs teach it how much: how bright, how loud, how wet, how far, or where a dial is turned.

title: Blink an LED with ESP32
desc: Your first real ESP32 project — one LED, a few wires, and code you write yourself. Let's make it blink!

title: Button Controls LED with ESP32
desc: Make your ESP32 react to your hand. In this mission a push button becomes an input, an LED becomes the output, and your code connects cause to effect.

title: Connect OLED Display with ESP32
desc: Serial Monitor is great for debugging — but a real screen on your project looks amazing. This mission adds a tiny OLED display that shows your own messages.

title: Button Debouncing
desc: Your button now has a stable default state. The next problem is speed: the ESP32 can see tiny mechanical bounces that your finger cannot feel.

title: Understanding Digital Inputs and Floating Pins
desc: Your button worked in Mission 02. Now you will learn the electronics behind it: why an input pin needs a clear HIGH or LOW, and why an unconnected pin can behave randomly.

title: Reading Environmental Sensors
desc: Embedded systems become useful when they observe the physical world. In this mission, your ESP32 reads air conditions and shows them on a local OLED screen.

title: I2C Communication
desc: I2C is the reason one ESP32 can talk to a display, a sensor, and other smart modules using the same two signal wires.

title: Installing Arduino IDE for ESP32: Complete Setup Guide
desc: Before you wire sensors or publish Wi-Fi dashboards, your computer must recognize the ESP32 DevKit and compile firmware with the correct board package. This guide walks through a complete Arduino IDE setup on Windows, macOS, and Linux—from download to a verified blink upload.

title: Multiple Buttons & State Detection
desc: One debounced button is useful. Real projects often need several buttons, and each one needs its own clean state, timing, and purpose.

title: OLED Display with ESP32
desc: Serial Monitor is useful while your ESP32 is connected to a computer. An OLED display lets your project explain itself without needing the Arduino IDE open.

title: Pull-up vs Pull-down Resistors
desc: You saw in Mission 03 that a floating input can behave randomly. Now you will learn the two simple resistor patterns that give every button a reliable default state.

title: PWM Fundamentals
desc: Digital pins can switch fully ON or fully OFF. PWM teaches the ESP32 to create useful in-between effects such as dim light and slower motor speed.

title: Read Temperature with DHT22
desc: Build a real ESP32 room sensor with the DHT22. You will wire power, ground, and one data pin, then read temperature and humidity in Arduino Serial Monitor.

title: Reading Analog Sensors
desc: A potentiometer is a dial you control by hand. Analog sensors use the same ADC idea, but the real world turns the dial for you.

title: What Is ESP32? Complete Beginner's Guide
desc: ESP32 is a low-cost, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth capable microcontroller that lets you build connected sensors, automation, and IoT devices without a separate computer on every node. This guide explains how it works, where it fits in your toolkit, and how to start your first project safely.

title: ESP32 AC Power Monitor
desc: Energy Monitoring · Measure real AC mains power consumption with an ESP32, ZMPT101B voltage transformer, and SCT-013 current transformer. Calculate Vrms, Irms, true power, power factor, and daily kWh, then publish results to an MQTT energy dashboard.

title: ESP32 AI Object Detector
desc: AI Projects · Run machine learning inference directly on the ESP32 to detect objects in a live camera feed. Start with simple colour-based detection, progress to TensorFlow Lite Micro person detection, and finish with a multi-class detector publishing JSON alerts to MQTT.

title: ESP32 Air Quality Monitor
desc: Environmental · Monitor indoor air quality with an ESP32, gas sensor, and fan or relay control — from a simple threshold alarm to a connected dashboard.

title: ESP32 BLE Beacon
desc: IoT Projects · Turn the ESP32 into a Bluetooth Low Energy beacon that broadcasts iBeacon or Eddystone-URL packets, enabling proximity detection, indoor positioning, and smart location-based automation triggers.

title: ESP32-CAM Face Detection
desc: ESP32-CAM · Use the ESP32-CAM module to stream live video to a browser and detect faces in real time using the built-in MTMN neural network, from a simple web stream to a full home security alert system.

title: ESP32-CAM QR Code Scanner
desc: ESP32-CAM · Use the ESP32-CAM camera and the Quirc QR decoding library to scan and decode QR codes in real time, displaying results on a web page, logging them to an SD card, and publishing them as MQTT access-control events.

title: ESP32 Camera Capture Server
desc: ESP32-CAM · Capture and stream images with ESP32-CAM — start with a local snapshot sketch, then add OTA updates and a web dashboard.

title: ESP32 CNC Controller
desc: Industrial Automation · Use the ESP32 with A4988 stepper drivers to build a CNC motion controller. Start with single-axis step and direction control, progress to a 2-axis plotter parsing serial G-code commands, and finish with a 3-axis CNC that accepts G-code uploads via a web interface.

title: ESP32 Digital Piano
desc: Education · Turn the ESP32 into a musical instrument using its built-in capacitive touch sensor pins as piano keys. Generate tones through a buzzer, then upgrade to I2S audio with a DAC for high-quality sine-wave sound, and finally add polyphony and MIDI output for a full synthesiser experience.

title: ESP32 Distance Monitoring System
desc: Sensor Projects · Measure distance with ultrasonic or ToF sensors on ESP32 — beginner breadboard builds through OLED feedback and tuned alerts.

title: ESP32 ECG Monitor
desc: Healthcare · Connect the AD8232 ECG front-end to the ESP32 to capture heart electrical signals, visualise waveforms in real time, calculate beats per minute, and stream live ECG data to a web dashboard for health monitoring.

title: ESP32 Fire Alarm System
desc: Smart City · Combine an MQ-2 smoke sensor and DHT22 temperature sensor with the ESP32 to build a fire alarm that sounds locally, displays alerts on an OLED, and publishes MQTT notifications to a smart-home hub.

title: ESP32 Gesture Recognition
desc: Sensor Projects · Connect the APDS-9960 proximity and gesture sensor to the ESP32 to detect up, down, left, and right swipe gestures and use them to control LEDs, RGB strips, and MQTT-connected smart home devices.

title: ESP32 GPS Tracker
desc: Sensor Projects · Connect a NEO-6M GPS module to the ESP32 to parse real-time coordinates, display them on an OLED, log tracks to an SD card, and transmit live location to ThingSpeak via MQTT.

title: ESP32 Greenhouse Automation Controller
desc: Agriculture · Automate a greenhouse or grow tent with the ESP32 by monitoring temperature, humidity, and CO2, then controlling exhaust fans, grow lights, and nutrient pumps on schedule and threshold. Scale from a simple climate relay to a full PID-controlled CO2 enrichment system with MQTT integration.

title: ESP32 Home Climate Automation
desc: Home Automation · Build a room climate controller that reads environmental data and automatically controls a fan, vent, or humidifier indicator.

title: ESP32 IoT Weather Station
desc: IoT Projects · Build an ESP32 weather station that measures temperature, humidity, and pressure, then reports clean readings for dashboards or local displays.

title: ESP32 IR Remote Control
desc: Home Automation · Use the ESP32 with IRremote library to decode infrared signals from any remote control, store the codes, and replay them to control TVs, air conditioners, and other IR-controlled devices — all from a web-based universal remote dashboard.

title: ESP32 Learning Trainer Board
desc: Education · Hands-on ESP32 trainer projects for classrooms — GPIO basics, sensor reads, and display output in three progressive stages.

title: ESP32 LED Matrix Display
desc: LED Projects · Chain MAX7219 8x8 LED matrix modules with the ESP32 to create a scrolling text display. Control the message and brightness from a web interface, animate custom patterns, and display live MQTT data as a real-time ticker board.

title: ESP32 Lightning Detector
desc: Environmental · Detect approaching electrical storms with the ESP32 and AS3935 Franklin lightning sensor IC. Measure estimated distance to lightning strikes in kilometres, filter false positives from indoor noise, and publish alerts to Telegram and MQTT the moment a strike is detected.

title: ESP32 Line Following Robot
desc: Robotics · Build a two-motor robot that reads line sensors and steers itself along a dark track.

title: ESP32 LoRa Remote Sensor Node
desc: IoT Projects · Use LoRa (Long Range) radio with the ESP32 to transmit sensor data over kilometres without Wi-Fi or cellular infrastructure. Build a transmitter/receiver pair, scale to a multi-node sensor network, and connect to The Things Network for global IoT coverage.

title: ESP32 Machine Monitoring Node
desc: Industrial Automation · Monitor machines with vibration or current sensors on ESP32 — bench alerts first, then local web status and mobile notifications.

title: ESP32 Motion Security Alert
desc: Security Projects · Build a motion alert node that detects movement with a PIR sensor and triggers a buzzer, LED, or Wi-Fi alert.

title: ESP32 MQTT Sensor Dashboard
desc: IoT Projects · Publish real-time temperature and humidity from the ESP32 to MQTT, then visualise it in Node-RED, Home Assistant, or a Grafana time-series dashboard.

title: ESP32 NeoPixel Music Visualizer
desc: LED Projects · A music-reactive LED strip that pulses and dances to audio — from a simple volume meter to a full frequency spectrum analyser with web-configurable colour themes.

title: ESP32 OLED Weather Clock
desc: Display Projects · Build a Wi-Fi clock that shows NTP time and weather-style readings on a crisp OLED display.

title: ESP32 Pulse Oximeter Logger
desc: Healthcare · Educational pulse and SpO2 logging with ESP32 — for learning only, not medical use. Progress from serial logs to connected monitoring.

title: ESP32 RFID Access Control System
desc: Security Projects · Build an RFID access controller that reads cards, compares IDs, and unlocks a low-voltage output for approved tags.

title: ESP32 RFID Inventory Tracker
desc: Industrial Automation · Track assets and inventory with the ESP32 and RC522 RFID reader. Scan MIFARE tags to log check-in and check-out events, maintain a stock count database on an SD card, and view real-time inventory status on a web dashboard — no cloud subscription required.

title: ESP32 RGB LED Pattern Controller
desc: LED Projects · Control RGB LED strips and patterns with ESP32 — simple color fades, manual modes, then Wi-Fi and app-style control.

title: ESP32 Robot Arm Controller
desc: Robotics · Control a multi-servo robot arm with the ESP32 using analog joysticks, record and replay motion sequences in NVS, and compute inverse kinematics to move the end-effector to web-commanded XYZ coordinates.

title: ESP32 Security Camera System
desc: Security Projects · Transform the ESP32-CAM into a fully featured security camera that detects motion using a PIR sensor, records JPEG snapshots to an SD card, streams live MJPEG video to a browser, and sends instant Telegram photo alerts when intrusion is detected.

title: ESP32 Smart Door Lock
desc: Home Automation · Build a safe smart lock prototype using ESP32, an access input, and a low-voltage lock actuator output.

title: ESP32 Smart Energy Meter
desc: Energy Monitoring · Track power usage with ESP32 and a current sensor — serial readings, calibrated LCD output, and cloud logging options.

title: ESP32 Smart Irrigation System
desc: IoT Projects · Build a plant watering controller that reads soil moisture and switches a pump or valve only when the soil is dry.

title: ESP32 Smart Mailbox
desc: IoT Projects · Add an ESP32 to your mailbox to detect when the lid is opened and mail is delivered. Send instant Telegram notifications, relay alerts via ESP-NOW to an indoor receiver, and run for months on a single battery using deep sleep.

title: ESP32 Smart Parking Sensor
desc: Smart City · Use HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensors with the ESP32 to detect vehicle presence in parking bays, display available space count on an LED display, and publish occupancy data to a web dashboard for smart city parking management.

title: ESP32 Smart Power Strip
desc: Home Automation · Convert a standard power strip into a smart IoT device using four relay channels controlled by an ESP32 web interface. Monitor current draw per socket with ACS712 sensors, schedule outlets on and off, and integrate with Home Assistant via MQTT.

title: ESP32 Smart Street Light
desc: Smart City · Smart street lighting with LDR or motion sensing on ESP32 — dusk-to-dawn control through connected city-style automation.

title: ESP32 Smart Thermostat
desc: Home Automation · Build a safe low-voltage thermostat prototype that reads room temperature and switches a fan or indicator when a threshold is crossed.

title: ESP32 Soil Moisture Monitor
desc: Sensor Projects · A soil moisture monitor that reads capacitive sensor data, alerts you when plants need water, and can automatically trigger a pump relay — with cloud logging at the advanced level.

title: ESP32 Soil pH Monitor
desc: Agriculture · Connect an analog pH sensor probe to the ESP32 to continuously monitor soil acidity. Display readings on an OLED, log data to an SD card, and publish MQTT alerts when pH drifts outside the optimal range for your crops.

title: ESP32 TinyML Sound Classifier
desc: AI Projects · Run lightweight audio classification on ESP32 — microphone input, on-device inference, and optional OLED or cloud feedback.

title: ESP32 UV Index Monitor
desc: Environmental · Measure ultraviolet radiation intensity with the ESP32 and VEML6075 UVA/UVB sensor. Display the real-time WHO UV index and risk level on an OLED, accumulate personal daily UV dose, and alert via Telegram when outdoor UV exposure reaches dangerous levels.

title: ESP32 Vibration Monitor
desc: Industrial Automation · Mount an MPU6050 IMU on rotating machinery and use the ESP32 to detect abnormal vibration levels, perform FFT spectrum analysis, and publish maintenance alerts over MQTT before failures occur.

title: ESP32 Voice-Controlled Relay
desc: Home Automation · Add voice control to any mains appliance using an ESP32 and INMP441 I2S digital microphone. Progress from clap-activated relay switching to on-device wake-word detection with ESP-SR and full Google Assistant integration.

title: ESP32 Water Leak Detector
desc: Home Automation · Protect your home from water damage with an ESP32 leak detector. Start with a simple moisture sensor and buzzer alert, scale to multi-zone monitoring with Telegram notifications, and finish with automatic main water shutoff via a solenoid valve.

title: ESP32 WiFi Robot Controller
desc: Robotics · Build a browser-controlled ESP32 robot that drives motors from Wi-Fi commands served by the board itself.
